field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: This is called the critical adsorption point (CAP), where entropic and enthalpic contributions compensate each other, and the interaction parameter equals zero.
field_evidence.critical_component: For functional polymers and block copolymers, the interaction parameter of the other block (or the end-group parameter) determines the elution behavior.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: critical adsorption point (CAP) for EO unit
conditions.physical_address: Institute of Chemistry, Karl-Franzens-University, Heinrichstrasse 28, Central Polymer Laboratory/Molecular Characteristics (CePol/MC), A-8010 Graz, Austria
conditions.notes: The text confirms that under these conditions (97 wt.% acetone on Luna HILIC), PEG elution is independent of molar mass (Fig 10), establishing the CAP for the EO unit. Other polymers like PPG and PTHF elute in SEC mode under these conditions.

conditions.notes: The text states a CAP for PEG was found on diol phases in about 80 wt.% acetone, but Fig 5 and the discussion of Fig 4 indicate separation according to hydroxy groups is achieved in 90-95% acetone on diol phases. The text explicitly links the separation of PEG derivatives by hydroxy groups on Nucleosil OH in 95 wt.% acetone (Fig 5) to the concept of suppressing molar mass dependence (CAP for EO).

conditions.notes: Fig 4 shows separation of PEGs and PPGs on LiChrosorb Diol in 90 wt.% acetone. The text states that under these conditions (similar to the 80% CAP mentioned), PPG elutes in SEC mode, implying PEG is at its critical condition (independent of molar mass).

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: under critical conditions for PEG on a reversed phase column in acetonitrile–water, acetone–water or methanol–water mobile phases both series elute as narrow peaks
field_evidence.critical_component: For functional polymers and block copolymers, the interaction parameter of the other block (or the end-group parameter) determines the elution behavior.
field_evidence.flow_rate: All separations were performed at a flow rate of 0.5 ml/min.
